key_insight: N-RSAV accelerates RSAV optimization by injecting randomized low-rank Hessian curvature while preserving the scheme’s energy-dissipation structure.
engineering_relevance: The method is useful for ill-conditioned PDE-constrained and PINN training problems where first-order optimization is too slow but stability must be maintained.
method: Build a Nyström low-rank approximation of the Hessian to form the RSAV linear operator.
Truncate negative eigenvalues so the approximate operator stays positive semidefinite and preserves dissipation.
Reuse the approximate Hessian adaptively when original and modified energies stay close, reducing overhead.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical tests on ill-conditioned, effectively low-rank problems such as convex quadratics and PINN training, showing substantially faster convergence than standard RSAV methods.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of the low-rank Hessian approximation and the extra PSD truncation/adaptive-reuse logic may add tuning complexity.
paper_title: Accelerating SAV-based optimization via randomized low-rank Hessian approximation
